description: Descend into a failing catacomb ward, put down the dead that stir from their niches, and re-seal the Circuit's keystone before the bindings collapse entirely.
customIntroduction: The stair the sage showed you goes down further than the chapter-house above has any right to. The air thickens with cold and old incense. At the bottom the catacombs open into a vaulted gallery of burial niches, the shadows between them long and unmoving. Somewhere deeper, past the reach of your lamp, something is failing — you feel the wrongness of it before the dark gives up anything to see.
customContext:
Beneath the chapter-house: the Circuit's keystone — the binding that anchors every ward — set into the floor of the innermost chamber, its glyphs failing. Senior Cape Edran Vael came down to hold the line alone and never came back up. The finale runs in three beats.

**Beat 1 — Discovery.** Reed explores the catacomb gallery; the ward's guttering light throws long shadows over carved names. Vael's body stays hidden until Reed has line of sight to it — it surfaces slumped at the stone the moment she can see into the keystone chamber — pressing SEARCH on it surfaces his field-note and licence-seal (don't request a Perception check; the SEARCH action owns the reveal). The binding rite is carved around the keystone's rim (Arcana/History DC 13–14, easier if she read the dedication stone last chapter): three passes of inscription, her own seal pressed at the close. It was written to be performed by a single Cape alone.

**Beat 2 — Combat.** Skeletons rise from the niches when Reed crosses into the keystone chamber. They are **vulnerable to bludgeoning** and **immune to poison** — her quarterstaff lands harder than her Fire Bolt, and the fight is built to teach that. Tense but survivable; the chamber gives her room to manoeuvre.

**Beat 3 — The Sealing.** With the dead down, Reed performs the rite at the keystone (3,10). She STUDIES the stone (Arcana) to read the Rite of Sealed Thresholds, then uses the **Magic action** on it to channel the binding home — that, not clearing the room, is what completes the chapter (combat-clear alone won't finish it while the rite is pending). The glyphs steady and warm, the cold lifts, the Circuit holds; she certifies the work in her own hand, with no senior to countersign. The cape, for the first time, fits.

This is a graduation, not a trap: a faltered check costs a resource and she tries again — don't let a bad roll close the door. The skeletons are the danger; the sealing is the ceremony. Vael is at rest — never hostile, never animated. His last act was to hold this line for whoever came next, and she is whoever came next.
objective: Reach the keystone at the bottom of the catacombs and renew its failing binding by hand — trace the rite, set your own seal, and certify the work yourself.

then.message: CLIMACTIC EXPLORATION + COMBAT — the finale. The intro is on screen: the long stair down, the cold and old incense, the gallery of burial niches, and a felt wrongness somewhere deeper. Don't re-describe it — open the moment and let Reed explore into the dark. Beat 1 (Discovery): secrets active, no combat yet. The keystone, Vael's body, and the rite are DISCOVERIES — do NOT describe the keystone or the slumped figure until Reed reaches the far chamber and the map reveals them (the SEARCH on the body surfaces his field-note). The skeletons are hidden in the niches — do NOT mention them or their positions until the combat trigger fires.

corpseSearch.successText: A field-note in a cramped hand, and the white cape itself — stiff with cold but whole. The note ends mid-sentence: '—recertification cannot be filed. The binding must be renewed by hand, by a living Cape at the stone. I have bought what time I—' You gather the cape from his shoulders; it is heavier than its weave should be.
corpseSearch.failureText: Vael lies as he fell — robes stiff with cold, one hand still on the stone. You find nothing on him you can use.

onPass.message: A faint scrape — bone on stone — carries from one of the niches on your left. Something in the dark has shifted. It is not the settling of old rock.

then.text: The dead stir. Skeletons come to life and attack!

then.message: THE SEALING IS COMPLETE. Close Beat 3: the glyphs steadying and warming under Reed's hands, the cold lifting, the ward locking back into itself. She presses her own seal to the stone — no senior beside her, no countersignature, just her mark and the silence after. Vael's hand has gone still; his line held. The cape fits. Award closing loot that fits the setting — his seal as a keepsake, a salvaged focus from his pack. For a colder last note: one wardstone on the Circuit remains uncertified, a hook for her next posting.

onPass.message: The binding-rite is carved around the keystone's rim — the Rite of Sealed Thresholds, three passes of inscription closed with a Cape's own seal. You can perform it here: channel the binding into the stone with the Magic action.

onFail.message: The rim is dense with worn glyphwork — a binding-rite of some kind, but its order escapes you on this pass. Study it again.

then.text: You trace the rite and press your seal to the stone. The ward locks home.
